answers.project_description: A full-stack restaurant platform for IMIDUS Technologies that integrates with a legacy INI POS (Point of Sale) system using direct SQL Server database integration. It includes React Native mobile apps, Next.js web ordering and admin portals, and a .NET 8 backend integration service that bridges modern applications with the INI_Restaurant POS database (source of truth).
answers.problem_statement: The restaurant currently runs everything through the INI POS terminal at the counter. There's no online ordering, no mobile app, no web presence for taking orders. This platform enables customers to order from their phones or the web, with orders appearing directly in the POS system as if placed at the terminal, payments via Authorize.net that post back to POS system tickets, restaurant owner to manage orders, run marketing campaigns, and view analytics, and loyalty points to work across in-store and online channels.
answers.target_users: 1. Customers using the mobile app and web ordering platform to browse menus, place orders, pay online, and earn loyalty points. 2. Restaurant owners/managers using the admin portal to manage orders, run marketing campaigns, view analytics, and control online menu availability. 3. POS operators using the existing INI POS terminal who receive online orders as regular tickets and process them normally.
answers.key_goals: 1. Enable customers to place orders from mobile apps and a web platform, with those orders automatically appearing in the existing INI POS system as normal POS tickets. 2. Build a secure backend integration service that bridges modern applications (mobile, web, admin portal) with the legacy INI_Restaurant SQL Server database without modifying the POS database schema or POS application. 3. Provide restaurant management tools (admin portal) for order monitoring, customer engagement, marketing campaigns, and loyalty programs while keeping the INI_Restaurant database as the single source of truth.
answers.voice_tone: Professional & approachable — clear instructions, friendly phrasing. Action-oriented — verbs first. Consistent brand — IMIDUSAPP capitalization, Brand Blue (#1E5AA8) and Brand Gold (#D4AF37). Positive & supportive. Customer-facing: short CTAs, positive success messages, constructive errors, branded notifications (IMIDUS | prefix). Admin portal: neutral, factual, instructional. Documentation: step-by-step, numbered, neutral tone.
answers.design_principles: User-centered, consistency & branding, clarity & readability, efficiency & learnability, safety & reliability, accessibility & inclusivity, scalability & maintainability, data-driven. Key elements: modular UI components, overlay tables for safe expansion, Brand Blue (#1E5AA8) and Brand Gold (#D4AF37), system fonts, actionable verbs, IMIDUS | notification prefix, real-time POS data display.
answers.tech_stack: Backend: C# .NET 8, ASP.NET Core Web API, Dapper ORM, SQL Server 2005 Express (legacy POS), SQL Transactions + Idempotency Keys, .NET Hosted Services, custom audit tables. Mobile: React Native 0.74.7, React Context/Hooks, TypeScript with theme tokens, Firebase FCM, Authorize.net. Web: Next.js 14, CSS Modules, React Hooks + Context, Authorize.net, AWS S3 deployment. Database: INI_Restaurant (source of truth), IntegrationService overlay tables. Payments: Authorize.net tokenization only. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, AWS S3, optional Azure App Service, MSI packaging for Windows.
answers.tdd_strictness: Strictly enforced for backend integration service (all writes to POS database, idempotency key checks, concurrency validations, transaction rollback scenarios), payment flows (Authorize.net, tblPayment posts), scheduled/future orders. Moderately enforced for web/mobile UI components (critical business logic: cart calculations, loyalty points, price/discount). Loosely enforced for static content/branding. Testing strategy: xUnit/NUnit for C#, mock Dapper repositories, integration tests against INI_Restaurant.Bak sandbox, regression safety requires tests for all POS table reads/writes, CI fails on test failure.
answers.commit_strategy: Branching: main (production, always stable), develop (integration), feature/<name> (per feature), hotfix/<issue> (urgent). Conventional Commits: type(scope): description. Types: feat, fix, chore, refactor, test, docs. Scopes: backend, mobile, web, admin, payments, overlay-db. Rules: atomic commits, TDD enforced (strict modules require passing tests), no direct commits to main (PRs only), PR review required. Tagging: v<milestone>.<subversion>, deploy-YYYY-MM-DD-<milestone>. CI: unit tests, integration tests, linting, build verification on every commit/PR.
answers.code_review: Mandatory for all merges to develop/main via PR (no direct commits). Backend/critical logic (POS DB writes, payments, scheduled orders): minimum 2 reviewers (POS domain expert + senior backend), focus on SQL correctness, transactions, idempotency, concurrency. Moderate risk (mobile business logic, cart, loyalty): 1 reviewer, focus on UI→API mapping, logic, TDD. Low risk (UI, CSS, branding): optional/peer review, focus on branding compliance. PR checklist: business logic correctness, DB safety (tested against sandbox, no schema changes, transactions with rollback), testing coverage, audit/logging. CI enforcement: merge blocked on test failures, linting errors, missing TDD.
answers.verification_checkpoints: Mandatory manual verification for: Critical backend writes (tblSales, tblPendingOrders, tblPayment, ScheduledOrders→tblSales injection), payment flows (Authorize.net tokenization, posting, partial/failed payments, Verifone/Ingenico bridge), cross-platform end-to-end flows (mobile→backend→POS→notification, web ordering lifecycle, admin portal scheduled orders), edge cases (max/min amounts, multi-item split-bill, loyalty zero/negative/exact redemption, kitchen routing flags, DailyOrderNumber midnight reset). Optional for: UI/branding only (visual QA), push notifications (device validation), reporting/analytics (spot-check). Checklist: reproduce workflow end-to-end, check data integrity (totals, counts, points, payments), check audit logs, validate external integrations, record pass/fail. Always document verification before milestone completion or S3 deployment.
answers.style_guides: C# (.NET 8): Microsoft conventions + project rules. PascalCase classes/methods, camelCase private fields (_prefix), Async suffix, IntegrationService.<Layer> namespaces, K&R braces, XML doc comments, Dapper parameterization (no inline strings). TypeScript (Next.js Web + Admin + RN Mobile): Airbnb + Next.js/RN rules. camelCase vars/functions, PascalCase components (filename match), use* prefix hooks, CSS Modules with theme tokens, @/ alias imports, single quotes JS/TS double HTML/JSX. React Native Mobile: PascalCase components, explicit types no any, StyleSheet.create with theme tokens, camelCase filenames, no magic numbers. SQL/T-SQL: Existing names exact (no schema changes), UPPERCASE keywords, PascalCase identifiers, explicit JOINs, transaction comments. JSON/API: camelCase fields, ISO 8601 dates, boolean true/false, plural array names. Documentation: Title case headers, fenced code blocks with language, consistent paths, left-aligned tables.
answers.existing_configs: Audit existing .eslintrc.js and .prettierrc.js — adopt for UI/mobile/web if they align with Airbnb/Prettier standards. Use .editorconfig for C# if it doesn't break Dapper/legacy SQL. Do NOT auto-format existing legacy SQL (preserve exact column names). Add project-specific overrides: theme tokens, absolute imports, async suffix enforcement, XML doc comments. Enforce via CI/CD: lint/formatting step must fail builds on violations.
